sections.paragraph:
γῆ, γῆς, ἡ [etym. unknown]

—1. earth, of the planet associated with humans Lk 21:35; Ac 1:8 and oft. in Ac; Hb 11:13; Rv 3:10, and freq. in distinction from its inhabitants; opp. οὐρανός Mt 5:18; 11:25; Mk 13:27.

—2. by synecdoche, w. focus on the earth’s inhabitants people, humanity Mt 5:13; Lk 12:49; J 17:4; Ac 8:33; 22:22; Ro 9:28; Rv 14:3.

—3. ‘a portion or region of the earth’, land, country, region Mt 2:6, 20f; Mk 15:33; Lk 4:25; J 3:22; Ac 7:3f, 6, 36.—Dry land as opp. of sea Mk 4:1; Lk 5:3; J 6:21; Ac 27:39, 43f. —As limited area for an action: ground, variously Mt 10:29; J 8:6; Ac 9:4; freq. of agricultural use Lk 6:49, with glosses soil, earth usually used for γῆ Mt 13:5; Lk 6:49; 13:7; J 12:24; Hb 6:7.
